name: Chocolate Triple Layer Cake with Fluffy Chocolate Frosting
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Grease (or spray with non-stick cooking spray) three 8-inch or 8 1/2-inch round cake pans.
3. In a small, heavy saucepan over the lowest heat, melt chocolate, stirring constantly until smooth. Set aside.
4. Sift together flour, cocoa powder, baking powder, baking soda, and salt onto a sheet of wax paper.
5. In a large mixer bowl, whip egg whites on medium speed until stiff peaks form.
6. Gradually add granulated sugar to the egg whites, mixing on low speed until combined.
7. Beat in the Egg, milk mixture, vanilla extract, and coffee until well incorporated.
8. Divide batter among pans, spreading evenly.
9. Bake in the middle of the oven for 23-28 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean but moist.
10. Transfer pans to racks and let stand completely cooled.
FLUFFY CHOCOLATE FROSTING:
1. Place egg whites in a large mixer bowl.
2. Set the bowl in a large bowl of very hot tap water and let stand for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
3. In a small heavy saucepan set over low heat, melt chocolate, stirring constantly until smooth. Set aside to cool slightly.
4. Combine corn syrup, 1/4 cup water, and sugar in a medium saucepan, stirring until well blended.
5. Bring mixture to a simmer over medium-low heat. Cover and boil for 2 minutes to allow steam to wash any sugar from pan sides. Uncover and continue simmering.
6. With mixer on medium speed, beat egg whites until very frothy and opaque.
7. Raise speed to high and beat until the egg whites just begin to form soft peaks; be careful not to overbeat.
8. Return syrup mixture to burner and reheat just to boiling.
9. Carefully pour warm syrup mixture into the beaten egg whites.
10. Beat in vanilla extract and coffee powder until well incorporated.
11. Sift powdered sugar and cocoa onto a sheet of wax paper.
12. Gradually whisk in melted chocolate into the frosting, incorporating smoothly.
13. Frost the cooled cake layers immediately or store frosting airtight for up to 48 hours. The frosting may soften and gradually deflate upon longer standing.
